evidence_used: support/checkers/profiles/core.yaml (lines 64-90: path_allowlist enumerating all 24 profile files)
missing_or_mismatched_facts: Analysis #1 (classify into the six named families: admission, boundary, runtime smoke, fixture, customer proof, reporter/dashboard/product projection): the inspectable structured 'returned' payload gives a flat 24-profile list plus a deterministic/live and four-llm-ownership narrative, but does NOT present an explicit per-family bucket mapping using those six labels; the 'customer proof' and 'reporter/dashboard/product projection' families are not addressed in the structured return, and the family taxonomy/ownership map is proposed as FUTURE work (Building B4) rather than delivered now. The output_excerpt is truncated, so I cannot confirm whether a fuller classification exists in untruncated agent prose (see not_proven).

Analysis #2 (heavy/duplicative profiles): heaviness is addressed but indirectly — the heavy candidates (building_skill_preset_agent_tool_hardening fixture corpus; coo_operating_chain text_contains pins) appear inside proposed_next_work_boundaries (B1/B3) and missing_scope rather than as a dedicated 'heaviest profiles + why' enumeration; the reasoning ('heavy' = file size + folded-profile count, not timing) is present and honestly caveated.

Task asked for 'disjoint write scopes'; the plan's B1 and B2 write scopes both include support/checkers/profiles/core.yaml and are therefore NOT fully disjoint. The plan itself surfaces this and recommends sequential ordering — recorded as an honest, reasoned deviation, not a concealed mismatch.
narrowly_proven: All 8 plan required_return_shape fields are present and the return carries no forbidden keys.

The plan's quantitative anchors verified against the cwd worktree: 24 profiles on disk match the listed names; expected_preset_count 28 with four-llm-standard-graph present; core.yaml path_allowlist enumerates all 24 profile files; check_profile.py enforces registry-closure self-test.
The deterministic-vs-live separation, four-llm ownership, and registry-closure coupling claims are grounded in the cited worktree source bodies I read.
Constraint adherence (read-only, no --all execution, no success/quality/Movement claims) is consistent with the returned payload.

Profile execution cost/timing and whether any profile makes a live network call — not executed (constraint); I observed only structural/docstring evidence, same limit the plan declared.
Source truth, success, quality, and Movement — not judged here; sufficiency and Movement belong to the Link gate, quality/approval to the human.
Semantic adequacy of the proposed B1-B5 write scopes and stop conditions for future Buildings — future Building correctness is not observable now.
